requirements.title: Scheduler Root Structure
requirements.description: The scheduler root shall hold a time source pointer, a flattened schedule table, apps-per-minor-frame count, minor-frame count, and minor-frame period.
requirements.rationale: The scheduler root holds all configuration needed to execute a time-triggered schedule: the time source for pacing, and the table layout defining which applications run in which minor frames.

requirements.title: Scheduler API Interface
requirements.description: The scheduler API vtable shall define Execute, GetMinorFramePeriod, and GetMajorFramePeriod operations.
requirements.rationale: Execute, GetMinorFramePeriod, and GetMajorFramePeriod are the minimal API surface needed to run and query a time-triggered schedule.

requirements.title: Schedule Table Layout
requirements.description: The schedule table shall be a flattened 2D array of application pointers with dimensions minor-frame count by apps-per-minor-frame.
requirements.rationale: A flattened 2D array avoids pointer-to-pointer indirection and dynamic allocation, keeping the schedule table in a single contiguous caller-provided buffer.

requirements.title: Execute Major Frame
requirements.description: The scheduler Execute operation shall invoke each application's OnProcess hook across all minor frames in one major frame.
requirements.rationale: Executing all minor frames in sequence completes one major frame, which is the standard time-triggered scheduling pattern used in flight software and real-time control systems.

requirements.title: Minor Frame Period Query
requirements.description: The scheduler shall return the configured minor frame period as a timestamp.
requirements.rationale: Exposing the minor frame period allows applications and diagnostics to query the scheduler's timing granularity without accessing internal fields.

requirements.title: Major Frame Period Computation
requirements.description: The scheduler shall compute the major frame period as the product of the minor frame period and the minor-frame count.
requirements.rationale: The major frame period is a derived quantity that callers need for cycle-level timing and telemetry; computing it from the minor frame period and count ensures consistency.

requirements.title: Time Source Dependency
requirements.description: The scheduler shall use an injected time module for timing operations.
requirements.rationale: Injecting the time source decouples the scheduler from any specific platform clock, enabling both real hardware timing and deterministic test clocks.
